workflow_name: Customer Inquiry Thread -> Response + Pipeline Note
user_message: Just came out of competitive positioning with Consolidated Messenger. Turn this into my brief and pull out the asks.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Sarah Singh <sarah.singh@consolidated.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-19 10:56 EST
Subject: Follow-up: competitive positioning Review

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with competitive positioning. What are the next steps from your side?

---
From: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
To: Sarah Singh <sarah.singh@consolidated.com>
Date: 2024-02-28 14:51 EST
Subject: Re: competitive positioning Discussion - Next Steps

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about competitive positioning. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Sarah Singh <sarah.singh@consolidated.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-10 12:55 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Consolidated Messenger competitive positioning

There's been a change in our timeline for competitive positioning. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

signals.detail: Signal detected for Consolidated Messenger: the cost projections need to factor in reserved instance pricing for accuracy

handoffs.condition: MSX update warranted from thread content

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF33-G trap (Draft response longer than thread warrants); produces complete Thread analysis, draft response, waiting-on-whom assessment, MSX pipeline note proposal (if warrante
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF33-G failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF33-G failure mode (Draft response longer than thread warrants)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct competitive positioning decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF33-G -- Draft response longer than thread warrants
